content_of_post_to_comment: The last time I was in therapy, my therapist didn’t seem to get the gravity of the situation with my father. She seemed to want me to stay in contact with him and my mum. She helped me in many ways, but she became just another enabler who I felt I have to convince that it was bad enough.
comment: One of the hardest parts of family estrangement is realizing the burden quietly shifts from proving harm to defending your perception of it. That can become its own form of exhaustion.
